column2: California man convicted of torture-murder dies on death row
column3: A 49-year-old inmate convicted of torturing and burning his victim alive has died on California's death row. Prison officials say Spencer Brasure was found unresponsive alone in his San Quentin State Prison cell shortly after midnight Thursday. Officials are awaiting an autopsy to determine the cause of death but say it doesn't appear to be foul play.

column2: Cherry disappointed with MacLean's apology: 'He buried me'
column3: Don Cherry isn't happy with former Hockey Night in Canada partner Ron MacLean. MacLean publicly apologized Sunday for Cherry's offensive comments from the night prior. Cherry, who was fired by Sportsnet on Monday, said MacLean buried" him with his response. "Very disappointing," Cherry told Global News on Tuesday. "We're still friends, but I was very disappointed in the way he handled it. I don't want to go and condemn him or anything like...""

column2: Suicide bomber attacks Indonesian police station, wounding 6
column3: MEDAN, Indonesia (AP)   A suicide bomber blew himself up at a busy police station in Indonesia's third-largest city on Wednesday, wounding at least six people. The attack came during a counterterrorism crackdown and as authorities were on heightened alert for possible attacks against police and places of worship following a militant attack last month.
